skill.name: freshservice-automation
skill.description: Automate Freshservice ITSM work through Rube MCP, including ticket search, ticket creation, bulk updates, service requests, and outbound email ticket creation.
skill.summary: Guide AI agents through Freshservice ticket and service request workflows using Rube MCP.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found they are false positives from Markdown inline code, Freshservice field names, and numeric status descriptions in SKILL.md. The remaining meaningful risk is legitimate but powerful Freshservice automation through an external MCP endpoint, including ticket creation, bulk updates, service requests, and outbound email ticket creation.

medium_findings.title: Authenticated Freshservice Write Operations
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to create tickets, bulk update tickets, create outbound email tickets, and submit service requests. These are legitimate Freshservice workflows, but they can change production ITSM records or notify requesters if used without user confirmation. Confidence: 0.88. Confidence reasoning: The documented tool sequences and required parameters clearly describe write-capable Freshservice actions, but no malicious intent or hidden execution path was found.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documented tool sequences and required parameters clearly describe write-capable Freshservice actions, but no malicious intent or hidden execution path was found.

low_findings.title: External MCP Endpoint Dependency
low_findings.description: The skill directs users to add https://rube.app/mcp as an MCP server. This network dependency is expected for a Rube MCP automation skill, but users should understand that actions route through an external service connection. Confidence: 0.72. Confidence reasoning: The hardcoded URL is explicit and relevant to setup, with no evidence of covert exfiltration or unrelated network destinations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The hardcoded URL is explicit and relevant to setup, with no evidence of covert exfiltration or unrelated network destinations.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Findings Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported external command detections occur on Markdown inline code and Freshservice tool names such as RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS and FRESHSERVICE_CREATE_TICKET. SKILL.md contains workflow documentation, not executable Ruby, shell, or script code. Confidence: 0.96. Confidence reasoning: Manual review of the cited locations shows backticks used for formatting identifiers and parameters, with no command execution syntax or user-controlled shell invocation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited locations shows backticks used for formatting identifiers and parameters, with no command execution syntax or user-controlled shell invocation.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Crypto And Reconnaissance Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and reconnaissance detections appear to match words such as description, desc, status, ticket_id, and email_config_id in Freshservice parameter documentation. No cryptographic functions, scanning logic, or reconnaissance commands were found. Confidence: 0.94. Confidence reasoning: The cited lines document Freshservice field names and numeric codes, and the repository contains only SKILL.md with no executable implementation files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines document Freshservice field names and numeric codes, and the repository contains only SKILL.md with no executable implementation files.

content.value_statement: Service desk teams lose time on repetitive ticket searches, updates, and request creation. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structured Freshservice workflows through Rube MCP.
actual_capabilities: Searches and lists Freshservice tickets with filters, sorting, pagination, and optional included fields.
Retrieves detailed ticket records when a ticket identifier is available.
Creates Freshservice incidents or service request tickets with required requester and priority fields.
Guides bulk ticket updates after listing and selecting target ticket IDs.
Creates outbound email tickets and submits service catalog requests through Rube MCP.
limitations: Requires an active Rube MCP connection and an authenticated Freshservice account.
Does not provide Freshservice credentials or bypass Freshservice permissions.
Depends on current Rube MCP tool schemas, so agents must search tools before use.
Bulk updates apply the same fields to all selected tickets.

use_cases.title: Triage Recent Incidents
use_cases.description: Find recently updated tickets, inspect details, and prepare prioritized follow-up actions.

use_cases.title: Apply Batch Ticket Updates
use_cases.description: Identify related tickets and update shared status, priority, group, responder, tags, or custom fields.

use_cases.title: Submit Catalog Requests
use_cases.description: Create service requests for catalog items with requester email, quantity, and required custom fields.

prompt_templates.title: List Recent Tickets
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Freshservice automation skill to list recent open tickets. Search Rube tools first, then show ticket IDs, subjects, status, and priority.

prompt_templates.title: Create an Incident
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Freshservice incident for this requester. Confirm the subject, HTML-safe description, status, priority, and requester email before creating it.

prompt_templates.title: Bulk Update Selected Tickets
prompt_templates.prompt: Find tickets matching these criteria, summarize the candidate IDs, ask for confirmation, then bulk update the approved tickets with the same fields.

prompt_templates.title: Coordinate Service Request Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for the current Freshservice service request tool schema, collect the catalog item display ID and custom fields, then submit the request after confirmation.

output_examples.input: Find urgent tickets updated since yesterday and summarize them.
output_examples.output: A concise ticket list with each ticket ID, subject, requester, priority, status, last update time, and suggested next action.

output_examples.input: Create a ticket for a laptop replacement request.
output_examples.output: A confirmation checklist covering requester email, subject, description, status, priority, and ticket type before the tool call is made.

output_examples.input: Update these five tickets to pending and add an onboarding tag.
output_examples.output: A pre-update summary of affected ticket IDs, the shared update fields, and a reminder that all selected tickets receive the same changes.

best_practices: Always search Rube tools first so the agent uses the current Freshservice schemas.
Confirm every write action with the user before creating tickets, sending outbound email, or bulk updating records.
Use pagination and narrow filters to avoid excessive API calls and incomplete ticket lists.
anti_patterns: Do not run bulk updates without showing the selected ticket IDs and exact fields first.
Do not use display names for custom fields when Freshservice requires internal field names.
Do not assume text labels for status or priority because Freshservice expects numeric codes.

faq.question: Does this skill include Freshservice API keys?
faq.answer: No. It requires an authenticated Freshservice connection through Rube MCP.

faq.question: Can it create and update tickets?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents workflows for ticket creation, ticket retrieval, and bulk ticket updates.

faq.question: Why must the agent search tools first?
faq.answer: Rube MCP schemas can change, so the current tool definition should be checked before each workflow.

faq.question: Can it send outbound email?
faq.answer: It can guide outbound email ticket creation when the connected Freshservice account has permission.

faq.question: Does it support service catalog requests?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes the service request workflow using item display IDs and custom fields.

faq.question: Is it safe for production service desks?
faq.answer: It can be used safely with confirmation steps, Freshservice permissions, and review before write actions.
